narrative.summary: You're in a small Brooklyn jazz club where the booking is serious and the room is intimate—you're close enough to see the player's expressions. No walk-ins, no exceptions: you need a reservation. This is the real thing, the kind of club that makes you remember why jazz clubs exist.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

6 Suydam St, Bushwick — the sign is subtle, the vibe is not.

You walk in and the piano is right there. A Bechstein Grand — the kind of instrument that serious jazz musicians cross boroughs to play. Named after "Ornithology," the Charlie Parker bebop standard, this place channels Bird's restless creative spirit. The room is small, the lighting is dim, and the musicians are so close you can hear them breathe between phrases.

blocks.title: Space
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘

Three floors of bohemian warmth:
• Ground floor: The bar and the stage. Intimate, standing-room vibe with some seating. The Bechstein commands the room ⭐
• Second floor: A lounge where you can play chess between sets
• Rooftop: Open air, for when you need to decompress from the intensity below

The décor is artsy — hand-painted signs, eclectic decorations, the kind of place where every surface tells a story.

blocks.title: Drinks
blocks.body:
𝗗𝗥𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦

🍸 Cocktails ~$15 each — classic and contemporary creations
• Classic cocktails done right (Old Fashioned, Negroni)
• Seasonal house specialties from the mixologist
• Beer and wine also available

The cocktails are described as "fun and flavorful" — built to complement a jazz evening, not compete with it. The price point is remarkably fair for the experience.

blocks.title: The Music
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗠𝗨𝗦𝗜𝗖

Live jazz every night, 6 PM onwards. Different bands rotate through — you might catch a trio, a quartet, or a solo pianist stretching out on the Bechstein. The programming leans bebop and post-bop but ranges across the jazz spectrum.

⭐ Friday/Saturday: Peak nights, cover charge applies (~$10-20), arrive early for a seat
⭐ Weeknights: More intimate, easier to get close to the stage

This is jazz the way it should be experienced — in a room small enough that the music wraps around you.

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 $

Cover: Free–$20 depending on night/act
Cocktails: ~$15
Evening for 2: $60-80

🌐 ornithologyjazzclub.com
📞 (917) 231-4766

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Daily: 6:00 PM – 2:00 AM

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Early arrival (6pm): Best seats, no cover on some nights
• Friday: The hottest night — expect a cover and a crowd
• Sunday: More relaxed, the musicians play looser
• Note: Address is 6 Suydam St (not the old 553 Gates Ave location)
